name: flask-alembic
documentation:
# Flask-Alembic

Flask-Alembic is a [Flask][] extension that provides a configurable [Alembic][]
database migration environment. Supports [Flask-SQLAlchemy],
[Flask-SQLAlchemy-Lite], or plain [SQLAlchemy]. Supports Alembic's single and
multiple database templates.

[Flask]: https://flask.palletsprojects.com
[Alembic]: https://alembic.sqlalchemy.org
[SQLAlchemy]: https://www.sqlalchemy.org
[Flask-SQLAlchemy]: https://flask-sqlalchemy.palletsprojects.com
[Flask-SQLAlchemy-Lite]: https://flask-sqlalchemy-lite.readthedocs.io

## Pallets Community Ecosystem

> [!IMPORTANT]\
> This project is part of the Pallets Community Ecosystem. Pallets is the open
> source organization that maintains Flask; Pallets-Eco enables community
> maintenance of Flask extensions. If you are interested in helping maintain
> this project, please reach out on [the Pallets Discord server][discord].
>
> [discord]: https://discord.gg/pallets

## Basic Usage

You've created a Flask application and some models with Flask-SQLAlchemy. Now
start using Flask-Alembic:

```python
from flask_alembic import Alembic

# Intialize the extension
alembic = Alembic()
alembic.init_app(app)
```

Commands are added to the `flask` CLI:

```text
$ flask db --help
$ flask db revision "making changes"
$ flask db upgrade
```

You can also access Alembic's functionality from Python:

```python
with app.app_context():
    # Auto-generate a migration
    alembic.revision('making changes')

    # Upgrade the database
    alembic.upgrade()

    # Access the internals
    environment_context = alembic.env
```

## Differences from Alembic

- Configuration is taken from `Flask.config` instead of `alembic.ini` and
  `env.py`.
- Engines and model/table metadata are taken from Flask-SQLAlchemy(-Lite) if
  available, or can be configured manually.
- The migrations are stored directly in the `migrations` folder instead of the
  `versions` folder.
- Provides the migration environment instead of `env.py` and exposes Alembic's
  internal API objects.
- Differentiates between CLI commands and Python functions. The functions return
  revision objects and don't print to stdout.
- Allows operating Alembic at any API level while the app is running, through
  the exposed objects and functions.
- Adds a system for managing independent migration branches and makes it easier
  to work with named branches.
- Does not (currently) support offline migrations. I don't plan to work on this
  but am open to patches.
- Does not (currently) support async engines. I don't plan to work on this but
  am open to patches.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.cli
documentation: Perform database migrations.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.mkdir
documentation: Create the migration directory if it does not exist.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.current
documentation: Show the list of current revisions.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.heads
documentation: Show the list of revisions that have no child revisions.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.branches
documentation: Show the list of revisions that have more than one next revision.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.log
documentation: Show the list of revisions in the order they will run.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.show
documentation: Show the given revisions.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.stamp
documentation: Set the current revision without running migrations.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.upgrade
documentation: Run migrations to upgrade the database.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.downgrade
documentation: Run migration to downgrade the database.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.revision
documentation: Generate a new revision.

functions.name: flask_alembic.cli.merge
documentation: Generate a merge revision.

classes.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic

type.name:
sa.MetaData
    | list[sa.MetaData]
    | dict[str, sa.MetaData | list[sa.MetaData]]

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.init_app
documentation:
Register this extension on an app. Will automatically set up
migration directory by default.

Keyword arguments on this method override those set during
{meth}`__init__` if not `None`.

```
param app

App to register.
```

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 ``run_mkdir`` and ``command_name`` are deprecated and will be removed in Flask-Alembic 3.2. Use them in the constructor instead.
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic._get_cache
documentation: Get the cache of Alembic objects for the current app.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.rev_id
documentation:
Generate a unique id for a revision.

By default, this uses the current UTC timestamp. Override this
method, or assign a static method, to change this.

:::{versionchanged} 3.0 Uses the current UTC timestamp instead of a UUID.
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.config
documentation:
Get the Alembic {class}`~alembic.config.Config` for the
current app.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.script_directory
documentation:
Get the Alembic
{class}`~alembic.script.ScriptDirectory` for the current app.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.environment_context
documentation:
Get the Alembic
{class}`~alembic.runtime.environment.EnvironmentContext` for the
current app.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.migration_contexts
documentation:
Get the map of Alembic
{class}`~alembic.runtime.migration.MigrationContext` for each configured
database key for the current app. Each context's connection will be
closed when the Flask application context ends.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.migration_context
documentation:
Get the Alembic
{class}`~alembic.runtime.migration.MigrationContext` for the current
app. If multiple databases are configured, this is the context for the
`"default"` key. The context's connection will be closed when the
Flask application context ends.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.ops
documentation:
Get the Alembic {class}`~alembic.operations.Operations` context for
each configured database key for the current app.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.op
documentation:
Get the Alembic {class}`~alembic.operations.Operations` context for
the current app. If multiple databases are configured, this is the
context for the `"default"` key.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.run_migrations
documentation:
Configure an Alembic
{class}`~alembic.runtime.migration.MigrationContext` to run
migrations for the given function.

This takes the place of Alembic's `env.py` file, specifically
the `run_migrations_online` function.

```
param fn

Use this function to control what migrations are run.

param kwargs

Extra arguments passed to upgrade or
downgrade in each revision.
```

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 Support multiple databases.
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.mkdir
documentation:
Create the script directory and template.

Alembic's `generic` template is used if a single database is
configured. The `multidb` template if multiple databases are
configured.

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 Support multiple databases.
:::

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.current
documentation: Get the list of current revisions.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.heads
documentation:
Get the list of revisions that have no child revisions.

```
param resolve_dependencies

Treat dependencies as down
revisions.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.branches
documentation:
Get the list of revisions that have more than one next
revision.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.log
documentation:
Get the list of revisions in the order they will run.

```
param start

Only get entries since this revision.

param end

Only get entries until this revision.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.stamp
documentation:
Set the current database revision without running migrations.

```
param target

Revision to set to.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.upgrade
documentation:
Run migrations to upgrade database.

```
param target

Revision to go up to.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.downgrade
documentation:
Run migrations to downgrade database.

```
param target

Revision to go down to.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.revision
documentation:
Create a new revision. By default, auto-generate operations
by comparing models and database.

```
param message

Description of revision.

param empty

Don't auto-generate operations.

param branch

Use this independent branch name.

param parent

Parent revision(s) of this revision.

param splice

Allow non-head parent revision.

param depend

Revision(s) this revision depends on.

param label

Label(s) to apply to this revision.

param path

Where to store this revision.

return

List of new revisions.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.merge
documentation:
Create a merge revision.

```
param revisions

Revisions to merge.

param message

Description of merge, will default to
revisions param.

param label

Label(s) to apply to this revision.

return

A new revision object.
```

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.produce_migrations
documentation:
Generate the {class}`~alembic.operations.ops.MigrationScript`
object that would generate a new revision.

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension.Alembic.compare_metadata
documentation:
Describe the operations that would be present in a new revision.

This only supports a single database. For multiple databases, use the
following instead:

```python
for ops in alembic.produce_migrations().upgrade_ops_list:
    name = ops.upgrade_token.removesuffix("_upgrades")
    diff = ops.as_diffs()
```

Provide an Alembic environment and migration API.

If instantiated without an app instance, {meth}`init_app` is used to
register an app at a later time.

Configures basic logging to `stderr` for the `sqlalchemy` and
`alembic` loggers if they do not already have handlers.

```
param app

Call init_app on this app.

param run_mkdir

Run  during init_app.

param command_name

Register a Click command with this name during
init_app, unless it is the empty string.

param metadatas

One or more  to inspect when
generating migrations. A single metadata or list will be assigned to
the "default" key. Or a map can be given that specifies one or more
metadata for each key. When using Flask-SQLAlchemy, db.metadata is
used if this is not given.

param engines

One or more engines to perform migrations on. Must match the
keys in metadatas. A single engine will be assigned to the
"default" key. Or a map can be given that specifies the engine for
each key. When using Flask-SQLAlchemy(-Lite), db.engines is used
if this is not given.
```

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 Added the ``metadatas`` and ``enginess`` arguments. Support Flask-SQLAlchemy-Lite and plain SQLAlchemy in addition to Flask-SQLAlchemy. Support multiple databases and multiple metadata per database.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 3.1 ``run_mkdir`` and ``command_name`` are keyword-only arguments.
:::

classes.name: flask_alembic.extension._Cache

methods.name: flask_alembic.extension._Cache.clear
documentation:
Clear the cached Alembic objects. Not all objects are cleared, only
those with connections that must be recreated on next access.

This is called automatically during app context teardown.
Cached Alembic objects for a given Flask app.
